name: Profinance
description_short: Profinance Federal Credit Union is a member-owned financial institution offering savings accounts, checking accounts, certificates of deposit, loans, and mortgages with 24/7 online banking access.
description_long: Profinance Federal Credit Union operates as a traditional credit union providing deposit and lending products to its members. Based on their website, they offer a full suite of banking services including checking and savings accounts, certificates of deposit at various terms, personal loans, mortgages, and wire transfer services. The credit union emphasizes member convenience through their 24/7 online banking platform accessible via computer, phone, or tablet. They also participate in the Working Advantage Savings Program, suggesting affiliation with workplace-based membership benefits. As a federally chartered credit union, Profinance operates under credit union regulatory frameworks and member governance structures. Their current rates reflect a typical credit union rate environment, with savings and checking accounts offering 0.10% APY and CDs ranging from 2.0% to 3.80% APY depending on term length. The organization appears to serve a regional membership base with phone contact at (219)736-5767, suggesting Indiana-based operations. What distinguishes Profinance is their focus on accessible online banking features including balance checks, internal and external fund transfers, loan payments, account history viewing, and recurring transfer setup. The credit union maintains straightforward fee structures and provides member support through both online and phone channels. As a credit union rather than a commercial bank, Profinance may offer lower fees and member-focused lending practices typical of the credit union model. An honest assessment reveals that Profinance is a standard regional credit union with competitive but not exceptional deposit rates compared to online-only institutions. Their CD rates are moderate for current market conditions, and their limited digital presence suggests they serve primarily existing members rather than aggressively recruiting new ones. Members should verify current rates and specific loan terms directly with the credit union before making decisions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Loan and credit card payment processing
pros: 24/7 online banking access across computer, phone, and tablet platforms
Ability to transfer funds both internally between accounts and externally to other institutions
CD special offering 3.80% APY for 12-month terms with $5,000 minimum (new money only)
Recurring transfer and payment setup capabilities for bill automation
Wire transfer services available to members
Share Draft (checking) and Regular Share (savings) accounts with low minimum ($5.00)
Member-owned credit union structure typically offering lower fees than traditional banks
cons: Deposit rates (0.10% APY on savings and checking) are significantly below current market rates for high-yield savings accounts
CD rates, while competitive for credit unions, remain modest compared to online bank offerings (2.0%-2.75% on standard terms)
Limited information on the website about loan products, terms, rates, and eligibility requirements
Regional credit union with membership restrictions likely requiring employment or residence in specific areas
No mention of mobile app features beyond web-based online banking access
best_for: Current members seeking convenient 24/7 online account management and fund transfers
Individuals with workplace or association eligibility for credit union membership benefits
Conservative savers seeking NCUA-insured accounts at a member-owned institution
Borrowers interested in exploring credit union personal loans or mortgages with potential member discounts
diagnosis: Profinance Federal Credit Union is best for existing members or eligible individuals seeking straightforward banking services with 24/7 online access and member-focused lending. The main caveat is that deposit rates are uncompetitive for rate-conscious savers, and membership eligibility may be restricted based on employment, residence, or association affiliation.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
